description: Target spot is a fungal disease of tomato that affects leaves, stems, and fruits and can cause significant defoliation under warm and humid conditions.
symptoms: Small brown leaf lesions that enlarge over time
Larger lesions may develop concentric rings
Lesions can merge and cause leaf blighting
Severe infection may cause premature defoliation
Fruit may develop sunken brown lesions

favorable_conditions.rainfall: Frequent rain can promote disease development
favorable_conditions.additional_conditions: Dense canopies with poor airflow increase risk

immediate_actions: Remove heavily infected foliage where practical
biological_control: Use locally registered biological fungicides where available

chemical_control.recommended: Use fungicides specifically registered for target spot on tomato when disease pressure warrants treatment.
chemical_control.warning: Fungicide resistance management is important. Rotate locally approved modes of action and follow product labels.

severity_guidance.low: Localized lesions. Monitor and improve canopy conditions.
severity_guidance.moderate: Multiple leaves affected. Strengthen cultural and registered disease-control measures.
severity_guidance.high: Widespread defoliation or fruit infection. Seek local agricultural guidance.

farmer_advice: Target spot may resemble early blight and other leaf spot diseases. Do not rely solely on target-like rings for diagnosis.
disclaimer: Image-based diagnosis may confuse target spot with early blight, bacterial spot, or Septoria leaf spot. Expert or laboratory confirmation may be necessary.
